I guarantee this fight will be a certified hood classic.

For some indescribable reason, the UFC wants to cling their hands to Tai’s pudgy waist so desperately that they not only dismiss his loss streak entirely as a very concrete reason to justifiably cut him from the roster, they gave him Sean ******* Sharaf. Sean is not a can, he the can of cans, the slobbiest of heavyweight slobs. He’s the equivalent of the fat chick in the group of popular mean girls in a high school: he exists solely to make other people look better. If a UFC contender’s skillset could be compared to an IRL profession, he is the skill equivalent of a **** set **** mopper. To go from fighting Cyril Gane to a human sceptic tank is such an embarrassing drop in quality competition wise that if I were in Tai’s shoes (preferably not the ones filled with ******* Blue Moon) I would hang up the gloves, immediately. posts.author: zeddhero
posts.body: Predictions: 13 of 13 Pending | Tied for 1st with 5134 others Della Maddalena vs PratesPick: Jack Della by DecisionJack Della Maddalena is a very, very, very good fighter and I think too many are forgetting how skilled he is because of that Islam fight. Yes, Islam wrestle-f*cked him the whole fight, yes, Islam got the better of some of the striking exchanges as well, but here Prates isn't even going to try to exploit some of Jack's weakeness on the ground. If anything, I think Jack may intiate some of the grappling exchanges later on. Prates has power with that left straight, he's put so many fighters out with it, but this is the type of fight Jack is made for. His top tier boxing, head movement, combos and IQ is going to shine big here, I could honestly see a masterclass from him here. These two have granite chins (especially Jack omfg) so doubt either man finishes the other, but Jack is going to have more moments and more strikes landed in order to win this one. Can't wait for him to remind the fans just how good he is.Dariush vs. SalkilldPick: Beneil Dariush by DecisionI get Salkilld is very very good and is a very promising prospect, but after seeing some of the trends lately in the LW division, and how good Moicano looked against Duncan. I'm starting to think there's still levels to this ****. Dariush's chin is made of glass for sure, but this guy's grappling and wrestling is still very slick, and rewatching some of the exchanges with BSD, he did land a couple of good shots before he got dropped and put out. If Salkilld doesn't put Benny away early, Benny is going to take over and show how valuable his experience truly is. I do like Salkilld a lot, perhaps my favorite LW prospect and this is a semi-favorable matchup, but I just think Benny is gonna win the grappling exchanges and outlast Salkilld to win a 29-28. * Edited at 04.05.2026, 10:42 AM ET *

posts.body: Predictions: 13 of 13 Pending | Tied for 1st with 5134 others Dariush decYeah sure, his chin is now composed mostly of styrofoam, but he’s a crafty man. The only reason he’s 1-3 in his last four is because his opponents have decent or huge power in their hands. Oliveria and Tsarukyan are the cream of the crop of LW, no shame in losing to them. BSD is a marauder who has nasty hands, a LOT of people would get colded had BSD not have the throwing speed of a slug. So what does Salkilld have in order to beat Benny? A finish over the Indian (look at him, he looks chinny), the Israeli (thats all I know abt him), and Nasrat (a decent win that is his best win). But Salkilld is going against the man who schooled Gamer man Gamrot and Money Moicano, two other crafty vets. I think its too early for Salkilld, as he’s someone I don’t think has the power to KO Benny nor has the skills to outgame him. Henceforth, it will be a Benny win by 30-27, 30-27, 29-28 Unanimous decision.

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Predictions: 13 of 13 Pending | Tied for 1st with 5134 others Tafa vs Christian Tafa KO RD 1 It seems the UFC has finally come to their senses and stopped trying to push Tafa on us; I was expecting this to be the co-main, considering how much they love him. I am no Tafa fan and I understand why everyone is picking against him, but to me this is a free win. I really hate saying that and acknowledge how gross this sounds, but Christian is one of the worst to ever do it. Tafa is a very bad mma fighter but he is a good striker; with better iq he actually would be on a 3 fight win streak right now, as he came very close to ko’ing his last two opponents. Now, this speaks to his piss poor grappling and all around low level skill, but the fact he is at least having moments against borderline UFC guys is enough for me. Kevin on the other hand, is truly a jobber. He looks serviceable on paper and I'm sure the main reason he’s so heavily picked here/lined closely is because of the 5 subs on his record, but that is a facade. Kevin doesn’t wrestle at all and in fact, he may just have a worse bottom game than Tafa. His dwcs fight was one of the worst looks I’ve ever seen, he was getting taken down by that jobber with no resistance and made no attempts to get up at all. These guys ground games are irrelevant here since neither will grapple, but it’s important to mention because that is literally the only reason this is a “close” fight on paper. On the feet, I feel confident that Tafa cooks him. Again, Kevin looks like a serviceable guy because of his size but he is a godawful striker. He has a very large frame but no idea how to use any of it. Kevin throws nothing on the feet, doesn’t find behind his reach, loops everything and has little power, and this is all topped off with tall man’s defense and a glass chin. Kevin was a retired fighter until 2023, and his two wins since then have both been comebacks where he was getting worked by jobbers until he found a finish. I expect this to end in r1 but even if it doesn’t, I actually think Tafa has better cardio. Now, I'm not going to pretend that Tafa has any form of good cardio, but Kevin has one round himself. Tafa is going to rush him on the feet and actually possesses decent boxing with great hand speed/power, and I think that’s more than enough to handle business here. Kevin is a very chinny jobber and isn’t even dangerous on the feet. I know how bad and unreliable Tafa is but I really feel confident this is a fight he wins. We see this often in the UFC but there is a stark difference between being a bad UFC fighter and a jobber who doesn’t belong at all near this level. Malkoun vs Meerschaert Malkoun KO RD 1 Malkoun wins this easily but this is a strange fight; I don’t like him at all but he deserved a bigger step up, my only assumption is that the ufc wants him to get a finish here. Malkoun’s finishing ability is some of the worst I’ve ever seen, dragging people with torn ACLs to a decision (twice now) and never pushing the pace. His skills are actually pretty good and you could argue he hasn’t lost since the Hawes fight, but he is a chore to watch because he never looks to hurt his opponents and usually just lays on them. This is a really weird fight because this is a huge step down in competition. GM3 was overrated even in his “prime” but now he’s just a literal corpse. There wasn’t a ton to like about him even years ago imo, other than his general craftiness and sub threat. In current form though, he is jobber tier. He does nothing on the feet and goes down to the softest shots. His grappling is kind of overrated too; sure, he is a very good submission threat but he can easily be taken down and held there too. I don’t see how Malkoun loses this one outside of some complete BS like the Brundage fight. He can easily outstrike GM3 on the feet and will get takedowns with no resistance here. Malkoun is one of the worst finishers I've ever seen but I feel like he HAS to get one here. Not even in terms of the UFC wanting it (although they legitimately may cut him if he gets another decision), but GM3 simply does nothing anymore. All Malkoun has to do is either rush him on the feet and show just a little bit of aggression, or take him down and throw gnp and he will easily finish this. GM3 will give his neck on a platter too, so maybe Malkoun can get his first sub. His finishing ability is so bad though that I’d be really shocked if he looks for one, I think a KO is more live. That being said, I really expect him to find a finish here. They are flying GM3 out for one last beatdown, and like I said he quite literally shows up to do nothing. Malkoun will likely win every minute of this fight, it’s up to him if he wants a bonus or if he shows up to lay on top/spar for 15 minutes. Thicknesse vs Morales Morales DEC Morales has a terrible record so I somewhat understand, but this line is pretty off to me; I’m not even sure I agree with Colby being the favorite. Morales is a journeyman but he’s not a can like his record would reflect. He was robbed against Lopez so he should be 4-7, and none of those 7 losses are bad. Morales has gotten cooked since coming back to the ufc but he’s had three really tough matchups. This is a big step down in competition and one that stylistically I see Morales having a lot of success in. I assume the reason Morales is a dog here is because of the takedowns, but I don’t think it’s going to be that easy to replicate what Rosas did. Morales does have bad tdd and loves to jump on the Peruvian/Japanese necktie attempts, but he’s hard to hold down and usually makes you work. Rosas was able to smother him for the entire fight but even then Morales threatened some subs and even took r3 off of him. That fight was not a great look in terms of this one, since Colby will spam takedowns but the grappling level of the two is wide. I don’t think that highly of Rosas but he’s a very good grappler and is ranked, so it’s not a bad loss. Colby may have grappling in his back pocket but he is not good at it. In fact, he’s not really good at anything; Colby is an exister. He doesn’t have any finishing ability nor does he use his cardio as a weapon, he just skirts on the outside, chipping at you and then spamming takedowns to try and get the fight down. I think he can ground Morales but holding him down won’t be that easy; not even because of Morales, I don’t think Colby’s grappling is even that good. His TDA is very bad (9% accuracy) and even when he gets you down, his control is nothing crazy. He was only able to hold Musasa down because he has no semblance of grappling and even then it took him forever to find the takedown. Musasa was honestly running away with the fight after a bad r1 but then Colby scored a td in the last 2 minutes of r3 that won him the fight. Musasa’s grappling made Colby’s much more effective than it ever has been; I don’t think he can mount Morales and hold him down the same way. If Colby can’t establish clear dominance on the ground, I don’t see how he wins this fight. Morales is a very beatable striker but Colby simply lacks the tools on the feet to outpoint him. His striking is not ufc level and he doesn’t do anything on the feet to win minutes. Colby is very passive and runs away the whole fight, having horrible reactions any time he’s forced to strike. He shells up when hit and only throws singular strikes at a time. Morales is limited in that he doesn’t check kicks and is boxing heavy, but his hands are fast and he will pressure you. He’s got pretty solid boxing by MMA standards and while he may be a bit of a punching bag, this is one of the worst strikers he’s ever fought in the UFC. That same logic applied in his last fight too but again, Colby’s grappling isn’t close to as good as Rosas’s. Unless he’s somehow able to drop Vince bad or cut him, I don’t see Colby being able to land enough volume and outwork Morales; just last year he gave an absolute dogfight to Smith on the feet. I’m very split between KO or DEC. I want to say Colby weasels his way to a decision like always, but you can’t keep getting hurt every fight and relying on takedowns. I know he survived Topuria but he fights very reserved, Musasa could’ve gotten him out of there with better tdd and I’ve seen Colby hurt many times on regionals as well. Morales is a bit of an idiot but he’s fighting for his job here against a very low level opponent (one of the worst he’s fought in the ufc). As such, I expect him to come out aggressive and I don’t think Colby is prepared for that style. He is going to need to wrestle his ass of from bell to bell and while possible, I don’t think his wrestling is really that good. He lacks physicality and even when on top he doesn’t do a ton of damage. This is really just an iq check for Morales and given the circumstances/his veteran experience I expect him to craft it up. I’ll go with DEC for now but I could easily see a KO if Colby can’t get this down. Schultz vs JohnstonSchultz SUB RD 1 I’ve been loving the randomness of these straight up signings recently, but Johnston is more in line with Julien Leblanc than a real talent. It makes sense that he’s the favorite given that Wes is complete garbage but as bad as it sounds, I’m pretty confident that Wes gets this done; or well, as confident as you can be in Wes Schultz. Wes is a bottom tier UFC guy but so is Johnston; he is another CKB charity case that they signed. It’s even weirder that they signed him now, Johnston hasn’t fought in two years because he retired, citing that the UFC was not interested: now they suddenly are? Doesn’t make any sense. He has a large gap in his career because he was competing in Muay Thai, but he didn’t compete at some super high level. He was the WBC world champ but I couldn’t find much of his record and he doesn’t appear to have any notable names; also because there's no talent at 185/205 in Muay Thai. He came back to MMA to help Izzy in camp for Poatan and jumped back into MMA. He is winning fights and it looks solid on paper but he is complete garbage. His wins are all against undersized MW who don’t know how to grapple. Despite getting finishes he is usually losing the fights until he isn’t, and all of his finishing sequences are just complete memes. On paper you see a striker who is getting wins by sub, but watching the sequences he’s not getting them because he knows what he’s doing; his last two sub wins were no hook rnc’s where his opponents gave their necks on a platter diving for takedowns. Johnston has been able to get to his feet fairly easily and even score some tds of his own, but it’s impossible to gauge because he is fighting complete jobbers. He may have some semblance of grappling but he can be taken down way too easily and in his last MMA fight he was getting out-hustled with wrestling until he found his signature meme RNC. Schultz is a very low level guy but the one thing he’s “good” at is wrestling. His technique is poor but the one thing I like about him, especially in this matchup, is that spams takedowns until he can’t anymore. They may be panicked, telegraphed shots but he gets most guys down; I think this successive attack should work here, especially since Schultz is the first warm body Johnston has fought in a while. Schultz’s grappling isn’t good either, and even in his wins he usually ends up getting reversed, but he is aggressive. When on top he throws a lot of gnp and is always looking for unconventional subs. They don’t work against real comp but he has multiple different wins by subs like Von Flue or Suloev Stretch, and I think that unorthodox attack could catch Ben off guard; he does not strike me as a competent grappler and already is not UFC level. Johnston definitely has the advantage on the feet but for someone who competed in Muay Thai his striking is pretty bad. He has okay technique but I didn’t see anything special on tape. He’s got some okay leg kicks and decent boxing but doesn’t seem very powerful. The only KO he has was an attritional beatdown against the fence against a small 19 year old Korean guy with 0 wins, so he hasn’t proven to me that he can KO real comp; he hasn’t so much as scored a knockdown in his other fights, and didn’t really score one in that fight either. Schultz is one of the worst strikers I’ve ever seen, so in prolonged exchanges I expect Johnston to find a finish. That being said though, Johnson does not have his usual big size advantage, and Schultz is good at landing on people despite his awful technique. Johnston relies heavily on his size in his fights and he is 2 inches taller here but doesn’t have the reach advantage. Schultz has no process on the feet but he will throw these weird, offbeat strikes and can land because he just bull rushes you. Weirdly enough, I think that might work here; Johnston has terrible tall man’s defense, was put out cold in his only loss, and has been dropped even in his wins. He’s chinny from his striking career and his ability to take shots isn’t even that good. I think Schultz is a bad striker but I wouldn’t be shocked at all if he catches him with say, a glancing overhand as he crashes in for a takedown. Schultz has bad cardio so I expect him to quit if this goes past r1 but even then, Johnston hasn’t fought someone who keeps the wrestling pace he does. I think Schultz has the ability to break him down with his grappling. As bad as Schultz is, you do have to factor in that he has much more experience, is much more active, 6 years younger, and is fighting a guy who quite literally was on the couch retired for the last 2 years. If Schultz loses this he should be cut before he leaves the country, It’s hard to have any faith in him but I think he can beat this level of comp. Johnston is as bad as it gets. Micallef vs Gorimbo Micallef SUB RD 2 Thankfully, it seems like the UFC has finally given up on Themba. He was always really bad but managed to overstay his welcome because the UFC thought they could make something out of him. I’m personally not that high on Micallef but I don’t see how this isn’t a really easy win for him. Themba has similar flaws to him and will fight to his strengths, so I see this being light work. Micallef’s striking is pretty flawed and overall I’m not sure what to think of it, but in this matchup he should be fine. Themba has a huge frame and is a decent athlete, but his striking is kind of clunky and he doesn’t trust himself. He does throw looping overhands to close the distance so he can then shoot, but his striking overall is not that advanced. I don’t really know what to think of Micallef’s striking, he too has a long frame and he has a really nice southpaw body kick, but at the same time he is predictable and gets hit a lot. The way he dismantled Jousset was very impressive but you also have to factor in that he had no idea how to adjust to the body kick, and is also the only guy who couldn’t knock him out. Micallef was also getting schooled in just about every exchange against Oban, so I’m not exactly sure he’s making strides there. Themba is an orthodox striker though, so Micallef’s signature body kick will be there all day. I don’t think we will see much striking here but if it does get extended it will be weird. I guess slight lean to Micallef as the younger, more durable guy but he definitely has some flaws on the feet. He’s very hittable as well but Idk how much that matters since Themba is as well. Themba has had some terrible reactions on the feet as well, so I wouldn’t rule out a KD/KO from Micallef. The reason I’m so confident in Micallef here is because Themba is going to force him to grapple. Much like Salkilld (who we will see in the co-main), I think the best part of Micallef’s game is easily his grappling, he just never chooses to go to it for whatever reason. We saw just last fight how good it is; Oban was smoking him in eveyr exchange and looking a career best, but after clinching with Micallef in r2, Micallef ended the fight within 30 seconds. The performance was kind of a red flag but the way he so easily turned the tables the second it hit the mat was beyond impressive; Oban wobbled him and clinched up, so Jono immediately got a body lock td and quickly got to the back for the sub. His scrambling ability is very slick and he is dangerous from all positions on the mat. I am a really big fan of his bjj and I personally think it’s very legit, he just shows no urgency to use it offensively. However that will not be a problem here since Themba will be shooting bell to bell. All themba knows to do is spam tds at range so he can lay n pray. Micallef’s tdd isn’t the best to me, but even if he gets taken down that’s actually a good thing here. I could totally see him getting a triangle/armbar off of his back, but Micallef’s scrambling game is very good as well. Themba will be able to get some control but he can be reversed and for being a primary wrestler, his bjj is actually very mid. Micallef is super dangerous on the mat and facing a white belt who will shoot on him, I have a veyr hard time seeing Themba stay safe. There’s also a chance that Micallef gets a td of his own; Themba will give him opportunities in just about every clinch exchange, and once on top this fight should be over very quickly. I don’t know what to think of Micallef overall but I think this is a complete layup for him. If Themba tries to wrestle he is going to find out very quickly that he is not on the same level when it comes to bjj here. Steele vs Mar Fan Mar Fan DEC This is an awesome fight to kick the card off. Really difficult fight to call because these two match up with one another pretty well. I’m surprised about the odds here too, I thought this would be pickem, not massively in favor of DMF. I don’t think Steele is very good at all but the jury’s still out on DMF and stylistically this is a close fight in general. I don’t think either of these guys are very good tbh, Steele is very overrated because of his bjj background and DMF is simply a weasel; he has a good skillset but he is gassy and thin margins, leading to a lot of very close, grindy fights. I would willingly fade Steele against most but I do take some pause here as on paper he is not a good matchup for DMF. DMF is a grappler who likes to grind you out in the clinch. He doesn’t care if he gets a td or has to cage push, he wants you up against the fence so he can grind you out. I think Steele’ grappling is very overrated but he does seem decently hard to outgrapple so I am curious. That being said, when you look into Steele’s bjj it’s actually pretty fraudulent. Sure, he has a lot of high level experience but he has no notable wins/tourney wins, nor was he a finisher in bjj. It’s translated very poorly in MMA as well, not only has his lack of submission threat carried over, but Steele’s tds and positional control are garbage as well. In fact, for someone as credentialed as him, his grappling tape is quite alarming. He only has a blast double as a td entry and even then it’s not that efficient. He has tried to grapple in most of his fights but his opponents have all easily countered it. The worst look to me was the Kilmer fight; Kilmer is a really solid grappler and regional fighter, but for someone like Steele this fight was a very bad look. Kilmer had the upper hand in the grappling almost the entire fight and arguably won as well; again, he’s a good fighter but for someone who’s BJJ is the only reason he has any hype, seeing him get clearly outgrappled with little answer for it was a very bad look. Even when Steele does get on top, he usually falls off/gets reversed/lets the opponent up; he does not look to control you or even look to finish with a sub. His tdd seems good and he is physically strong/the better athlete, but I honestly kind of think DMF can hustle him on the mat here. His control isn’t the best but he has shown really good scrambling and good top control. Steele will resist but his wrestling is honestly pretty fraudulent to me. I think DMF will struggle a bit but his constant cage pushing/chain wrestling should allow him to get the fight down. Steele is much more credentialed on paper but again, I think his BJJ is really quite fraudulent. DMF can be subbed/reversed/taken down, but he has good hustle and again, is just a weasel; he always finds his way up to his feet. I want to say Steele can negate the grappling and even get control of his own but again, his bjj is really suspect to me. For all his flaws, DMF can wrestle his ass off and even if he slows, he pushes at a hellacious pace. Steele is also an ape (meaning he is mindless and has poor decision making), which I think will work against him because he just wants to bang it out, where DMF will do everything in his power to smudge you. Steele will give openings for takedowns and his overall MMA iq seems to be lacking. Steele has also taken 14 months off from his debut with 0 bookings, which is kind of a red flag to me. He turns 32 later this year so he is a finished prospect and I don’t think we see any improvements from here; this was very concerning to see because I thought Steele was still in his 20s. Steele is actually a better striker here but I hate his process. For a bjj guy who was a pure grappler, his hands are really good. However for the UFC level, his striking sucks. Steele does have a ton of power and I love his aggression (very uncommon for a bjj guy), but his technique is not UFC level. He throws only hooks on the feet and his aggressive style leads to him getting hit a lot. He got completely outclassed by Rongzhu and even on regionals would get hit a lot on the feet before getting finishes. DMF’s striking is pretty bad and he is very hittable/uncomfortable on the feet, so Steele’s aggressive style could be huge here. I think DMF can land easily on Steele here, but I am worried since he’s going to fight up close, where Steele is at his best. DMF is very susceptible to body shots and does gas, which is Steele’s best weapon on the feet; so I am kind of worried he can cook DMF in the clinch. That being said though, I ultimately feel like DMF weasels his way to a win again. The striking is really bad for DMF but I think he will be able to mix in takedowns and his pace/weasely style will pose a lot of issues. I can definitely see Steele winning as he’s the better striker and has the grappling (on paper) to negate the takedowns, but watching the tape back I kind of think Steele is a fraud. His bjj is really not as good as it seems and without that he’s just a brawler with some above average athleticism and good pace. I don’t like DMF at all and his time is coming very soon, I’m just not sure if it’s here. Funny this is the curtain jerker because this is the fight I just can’t wrap my head around. * Edited at 04.26.2026, 11:06 AM ET *

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Predictions: 13 of 13 Pending | Tied for 1st with 5134 others Della Maddalena vs Prates Della Maddalena DEC This is such a sick fight, the best main event of the year so far and one of the best bookings of the 2020s imo; two of the more dangerous and exciting strikers in all of the UFC going at it. JDM did lose some shine against Islam but against a pure striker and in his backyard, I expect the best version of himself. Prates on the other hand is simply must see TV. He’s one of the most dangerous strikers in all of the UFC and has already amassed an all time highlight reel. Anyone who is going to stand with Prates the entire fight is in for a long night. Prates can be outstruck and is hittable but he carries his power the entire fight and all he needs is one opening. His frame is massive and he is a sniper; he picks his shots well and is a master of finding openings. His last fight is a perfect example, he was completely out of place and Leon thought he was safe, yet Prates caught him on the literal tail end of his punch; that shouldn’t have dropped him let alone KO him but Prates’s power is just that scary. We did see him freeze up a little against Garry, and while Garry fought amazing I also think part of that was Prates freezing in the spotlight a little; he’s been more aggressive since then and looked as scary as ever. As much as I’m glazing Prates, I actually think this is a tricky fight for him. Not like JDM has an easy fight in front of him either but stylistically I think he has a great style to cause issues here. For one, JDM is not going to stand at range and let Prates cook. Prates has the reach advantage and is so good at using his kicks/reach on the outside, but JDM likes to fight in the pocket. Brawling up close with Prates seems like an awful idea, but it takes away his reach advantage and imo a little bit of the KO threat. Prates is still dangerous but he needs range to fully load up his killshots, meeting him in the pocket takes that away, and I also don’t think Prates is as comfortable in pocket exhcanges. He especially won’t be here, as JDM is an elite pocket fighter. His boxing is textbook and he is so good up close; always slipping shots, ripping to the body aggressively, throwing 10+ hit combos, etc. Both of these guys are elite strikers in their respective disciplines, so I think whoever gets the fight where they want will fare well. Prates loves a scrap but imo JDM is more comfortable in those kind of fights. Prates can keep up for sure, and even though JDM is hittable I weirdly kind of think he stays more composed in firefights. We’ve seen that Prates can get lazy with his guard and up close I do think he’s at a sizeable disadvantage here. The public has seemed to be pretty low on JDM ever since the Hafez fight but he’s still one of the best boxers in the UFC imo. He can take a shot and despite fighting in the fire, he stays defensively sound with a tight guard and elite pocket boxing. I think he can crowd the space here, take away the range, and he easily has the cardio to fight at a high paced brawl the entire time. It’s very scary because Prates can kill you at any moment, but imo JDM is the better minute winner and I think his output will be hard to deal with. Prates will be able to land on him but I think he will fall behind when JDM gets into the pocket and rips combos. I expect Prates to be more prepared for a 5 rounder this time, and I also don’t think he has cardio issues, BUT I do think JDM’s pace will be a problem here. Prates isn’t a gasser imo but we did see him slow down hard in the champ rounds against Garry, and JDM is an aggressive body puncher. His style is taxing on cardio anyways and with the body work/pace, I think we could see Prates slow down here. THis is a very close fight and I hate going against Prates, but I think JDM is a tough matchup for him. I know a lot of people are saying this ends in a KO but my hot take is that this goes the distance. I don’t think Prates is a quitter nor is he fragile like his record implies, and JDM is insanely tough himself. JDM also stays defensively sound and is elite at rolling with strikes, so I think he will stay safe from the killshot. THis is honestly the one time I’m hoping a fight goes the distance, if we get 25 minutes of this we just may get one of the best striking matches of all time. Dariush vs Salkilld Salkilld KO RD 1 Predatory matchmaking here. I do take some pause as we’ve seen the vets have been crafting it up lately, but Beneil seems completely done. I will say, I think Quillan is super overrated and on paper this is a terrible style matchup for him. If Beneil doesn’t get chinned early he could easily outskill Quillan here. Glass chin aside though, I’m not sure if Beneil has another war in him. I think even in current form he’s still more skilled than Quillan, but we’ve seen a physical decline as well. Beneil missed weight against BSD which was a pretty bad red flag to me; he’s always had a tough cut and even if marginal, missing weight is usually a sign that you’re just done. Beneil is a professional, so it was not an intentional miss; I think his body was just rejecting the cut. Stylistically Beneil will not get outgrappled here and I think he’s the better striker, I’m just not sure he can keep up with Quillan’s pace. I’m not even talking about cardio, moreso the chaos that Quillan brings. He goes 12/10 in terms of pace and is in your face brawling or spamming takedowns. Beneil is a better technician but in a chaotic brawl it’s impossible to trust his chin; especially against the much younger, more durable guy in his physical prime. Quillan is sloppy defensively and not even that good of a striker imo, but he is huge, powerful, and going to get right in Beneil’s face. Beneil will be able to land but he doesn’t have great power and his chin is just shot to pieces. Having a negative KO record is bad enough, but Beneil has sooooooo much mileage. His 6 KO losses are all vicious as it is, but he’s been ko’ed even more times in sparring. This is something his coach confirmed after the Arman fight, and Kings MMA is known for Chute Boxe style sparring with no headgear, so Beneil’s chin is just completely toast. He will be prepared for the chaos Quillan brings, but his body will not be. I don’t even expect Beneil’s cardio to fail nor do I expect him to get outgrappled, it’s just so tough for me to see him avoiding the killshot against someone who is going to be in his face the whole time. We did see Quillan slow down against Yanal but even then he fights through it well; he’s not going to back off on the pressure even if he slows here. I could possibly see this get extended past r1 but even then I wouldn’t feel good about Beneil; If anything, it’s scarier because he would need a perfect 15 minutes without getting clipped. Quillan usually has his wrestling/grappling to rely on but that won’t be relevant here as he is not outgrappling Beneil; nobody’s been able to do it before and even in current form, he’s still near impossible to outgrapple. Quillan could ground him for sure but I would be very shocked if he can do anything more than a few seconds of control; Beneil would either have to be physically shot to pieces or compromised. Quillan is still a very green striker who has a lot of defensive woes, but I don’t think that matters here. What matters is that he will pressure hard and can eat the shots of Beneil; without respect, I think Beneil gets walked down and chinned again. He is someone who probably should not be fighting anymore. We saw against Moicano the skills are still there, but Beneil’s chin is like 60 in fight years. Elliot vs Erceg Erceg SUB RD 3 Good flyweight scrap, Erceg finally gets his hometown fight in Perth. I’ve always been pretty low on Erceg but I think this is one he should easily win. I know Tim had that big upset last fight but I think that was more due to Kai struggling than Tim doing well; on rewatch Tim was getting lit up before he got the takedowns going. Kai also has negative BJJ so can’t take much from that. I am not a huge Erceg guy whatsoever but stylistically I think this is a pretty good fight for him. I kind of think he’s better everywhere, and he’s got a good deal of experience now so Tim can’t even rely on vet savvy. Tim has a very unique style that’s hard for anyone to prep for, but Erceg has him covered on the feet and I think he’s the better grappler. Tim’s style is so weird to get a read on because he’s so unconventional; everything is thrown with offbeat timing and at weird angles. Tim is always good at evening up the strikes but the caveat of this style is that he has no power and gets hit a lot; it’s pretty ineffective against guys with good fundamentals like Erceg. Erceg’s biggest flaw is his terrible tall man’s defense but he should be fine here since Tim doesn’t hit hard. Erceg’s boxing is pretty solid and he has a big frame which helps. He is kind of bad at separating but I don’t think Tim can match his output. Tim’s striking is great against unproven guys since he’s so hard to prep for but against good technicians I don’t think it’s super effective; he does even up the strike count but his volume is relatively ineffective. Tim is a better wrestler but I think it may work against him here as Erceg’s bjj is the best part of his game imo. Erceg never really uses it offensively but I think he has good control and scrambles really well. I know that sounds false considering how mid he looked his last fight, but I think a big part of that was due to Erceg being hurt in r1 and just trying to get the win more than impress. Tim is a better wrestler and will be able to hold Erceg down, but I think Erceg will be able to get to his feet fairly easily or end up on top. He moves very well on the ground and is really hard to hold down. The only guy who had success with this was Pantoja, and we all know how competitive that fight was. I also still think Elliot is washed despite his win over Kai. That was an awesome upset but imo he really didn’t look that great. I thought he was looking pretty mid on the feet and only started working Kai once he got the fight down. I think that win will age decently well but Kai still has to find his footing in the UFC; also not really relevant in this fight since Erceg is an infinitely better grappler than Kai. We also have to remember that Elliot is still 39 years old and had that brutal knee injury. Erceg has been very mid since the Pantoja fight but I think he has the skillset to cruise here. He should be a step ahead on the feet and while I expect Tim to get him down and make it scrappy on the mat, I think Elliot is the better grappler. Tim is a great wrestler but he does get sloppy on the mat and has numerous losses where he gets ****y and puts himself into a sub. I think this probably goes the distance but since I expect a lot of grappling here, I think Tim may get nonchalant and find himself stuck in something. Erceg isn’t some pace god but Tim has gassed himself out numerous times and I don’t really trust his cardio at 39. Gaziev vs PericicPericic KO RD 1 Big push for Brando but I guess this is how the HW division is these days; if you show any promise you get immediately pushed to top 15. I normally don’t like it because these guys usually suck/aren’t ready, but Brando is a really good prospect imo. The latter may be true here since he’s inexperienced, but I think Brando’s skillset is great. He’s a giant heavyweight but a great athlete and moves incredibly fast for the division. He has a background in K1 and trains at CKB but what I like about him is that he doesn’t fight like his teammates; all the CKB guys fight very composed but Brando has a good blend of staying technical but keeping that classic HW brawler mentality. He can be taken down but like all CKB guys, Brando has shown very good tdd and anti-grappling. He did get subbed in his lone loss but Brando was defending teh grappling pretty well until he slowed. I think Brando’s skills are very good and he has the physical intangibles needed to succeed at the highest level, my issue with him is cardio. I don’t even necessarily think he has bad cardio, it more so has to do with how he paces himself. I think it speaks to his relative inexperience but Brando fights with an almost nervous energy every fight; he is there for a quick KO and fights like it every time. He comes out like a bat out of hell and is kill or be killed, his lone loss came this same way. I don’t think it matters here since I expect this to end quick but whenever Brando does lose I think it will come from cardio management. I love me some Gaziev and have been partial to backing him, but I sadly think this is a very bad matchup for him. Gaziev has an underrated skillset for HW but his achilles heel is that he’s a fat blob; he has no athleticism or movement, and gasses hard. Against other blobs I love backing him, but I’m worried he gets smoked here. The reason being, the speed/athleticism gap is just crazy here. Brando is a top tier athlete for HW imo, where Gaziev is stuck in the mud and porky. I’ve always thought Gaziev’s power is crazy underrated and he has decent boxing, but his hand speed is slow and his output limited. Gaziev also does not react well to being hit and has bad defense himself. Brando gets hit but he has good defense and much better kickboxing; he has a lot of pro K1 experience and it shows. He is going to be so much faster than Gaziev here and I think he will find the chin fairly easily. Gaziev is not that durable and even though I love him, he’s quit in both of his losses. He is going to absolutely hate getting hit by Brando and I think the speed gap is going to be a nightmare for him. I don’t see Gaziev having much wrestling success here either. Besides having good tdd to begin with, Gaziev is too slow to get to the hips here. I don’t think he can react in time, and the pressure/speed of Brando will make it difficult to establish wrestling as it is. Even if he gets takedowns, Gaziev just blobs on top so Brando should be fine. Brando’s cardio is definitely not great but Gaziev’s is just as bad if not worse. He has a good 6-7 minutes in him and then completely gasses out. I don’t think it matters here since I expect this to end early, but if this does go past round 1 it just comes down to who wants it more; it’s gotta be Brando in that case. Gaziev completely goes into cardiac arrest when tired and doesn’t do anything, where Brando goes out on his shield. I love Gaziev but he’s really old and I think he has sadly ran his course. I’m pretty high on Brando and I think while fresh he is an absolute nightmare style for big Gaziev. Tuivasa vs Sutherland Tuivasa KO RD 1 Kind of crazy that fat blob Tui is getting yet another fight but at least this should be fun; UFC should just give him equally as fat blobs until he retires, no more ranked guys. Tai as a big favorite in the big 2026 is just disgusting but I feel like he should win this. That fight with Xicao was one of the lowest skilled fights in a while but at least Tai put up a competitive effort. We know his grappling is garbage but while fresh he at least got up and even while exhausted he still won r3; he may be done but he at least showed he’s got something left. His skillset is terrible and all he is is a fat brawler with 1 round of cardio. It’s really hard to justify picking him against anyone, but Louie is just a can. The only reason the UFC signed him was to take fights like these, and he is collecting one last paycheck; he got ko’ed last month in a pretty pathetic quit job and is now flying across the globe on 2 weeks notice. Louie will wrestle here which is my main worry, but watching his tape he doesn’t really do anything with it. Tai is one of the worst grapplers in the ufc but seeing him survive those bad spots against Xicao and getting to his feet twice in r1 tells me that he should be fine here. Xicao is really bad but he is at least a black belt (fraud or not it’s more than Louie) and a massive human. Louie will shoot takedowns but he only has a rugby tackle and once on top he doesn’t really dfo anything. If he was active with gnp or at least had some semblance of a submission I would be tempted to pick him, but I think Tai should be able to get up. If Louie can’t dominate him on the ground and get him out of there early, he is going to give up. I think Brando is a beast and I will talk about him next fight but that performance from Louie was nothing short of pathetic; he came out hot with a blast double and after Brando got up, he quit 30 seconds later. Besides going down to easy shots, Louie had about a 60 second gas tank before he called it quits. Tai’s cardio is horrible too but he at least showed a will to win the last fight, Louie is looking for the door once he slows, and his cardio genuinely might be worse than Tai’s. On the feet Tai should be fine here, Louie doesn’t hold any advantage in terms of technique/speed/power, and he hates getting hit. I think this should be an easy KO 1 for Tai. Nothing would shock me with him anymore but Louie is truly a terrible fighter and is flying across the world on short notice so he can get paid. His last performance tells me that he is going to quit the second things start to go south. Rowston vs Bryczek Bryczek KO RD 1 This is one of the fights I’ve struggled the most with. I thought Bryczek was a can before his last fight but watching him back, he is decent. A very flawed fighter and still pretty limited at the end of the day, but he isn’t a bad fighter. Rowston is a weird case, I think he’s improved a lot since his first DWCS fight but it’s hard to say exactly how good he is. It also isn’t difficult to build off of who he was when he fought Finney either; Rowston was not an active fighter and looked like a complete jobber in that fight. Since then he has improved his output, gained more confidence on the feet, and seems to have improved his grappling somewhat. He’s also being active and is on the CKB juice, which does help. That being said, he is fighting the worst of the worst. He is picking up finishes but his fights in Eternal were pretty ugly and he showed a lot of his old self against Brundage. Brundage was throwing awful hooks with no setup but was landing easily on Rowston, even hurting him pretty good in r1. Brundage did his usual quit job as the fight got extended but even then he was still having some success in r2. I think Rowston has more tools and ways to win here, but I also really doubt his level; I doubt the level of both honestly but at least Bryczek has beaten someone with a pulse. The striking is going to be pretty ugly here since both have holes that can be exploited. I think Bryczek is a much better boxer, the problem is that boxing is all he does. If he pressures and gets in the pocket, I kind of think he will smoke Rowston: his hand speed is much faster, he will be able to rip the body freely due to Rowston’s size (he also slows), and Rowston has a suspect chin/bad defense. Bryczek pressures well and while basic, his boxing fundamentals are very solid. He fights behind only straights and when up close he rips to the body/head with flurries of hooks. Rowston’s striking is okay but he doesn’t have a lot of power and has really bad tall man’s defense. He has improved his confidence a ton (used to be terrified of everything) but it’s still really hard to rate his improvements because he’s fought complete cans. I do like his kicks/knees and he has started to use his size well, but Rowston still struggles in the pocket and has not fixed his issues when pressured; specifically when put on the backfoot. My problem with Bryczek is that when he can’t pressure he is lost. He showed a lot of improvements against Tavares but after a good r1 we saw he was still behind on a technical level and having trouble when Tavares was leading the dance. This worries me a little bit because Rowston does have solid technical abilities and a large frame; I can see a world where he circles on the outside picking Bryczek apart with kicks and chipping at him with his jab. That being said, Rowston is so bad on the backfoot that I think Bryczek can pressure here. He walks you down in every fight and Rowston has not shown great cagecraft/defense at all. I think Bryczek will be able to land on him and he definitely has the ability to end it. Rowston’s chin is a little sus to me, he’s been put out cold before and dropped many times. The Brundage fight was a pretty bad look and he was hurt really badly in r1. If Rowston grapples it could get interesting here. Bryczek’s tdd is okay but he is a bad grappler. Despite the subs, Rowston is a pretty bad grappler himself. He does have some understanding of what to do but he moves incredibly slow and could barely hold the jobbers on the regional scene down. His td entries are really bad as well, his nonexistent athleticism makes them even worse. Bryczek’s ground game is sus but I’m honestly not sure Rowston’s wrestling is good enough for it to matter; especially since Bryczek will be pressuring. If this gets extended I have to favor Rowston but it will be interesting because I don’t think either has great cardio. I was impressed with how Bryczek kept pushing and got the r3 KO, but I also think a lot of that was due to Tavares’s style letting him back into the fight; he dropped r2 and was looking pretty deflated despite the forward pressure. Rowston def has better conditioning but he too slows down hard. I think he fights through it better but that won’t matter if Bryczek is able to walk him down. This is a really ugly fight and I think the odds are kind of crazy; Rowston is not good enough to be this massive favorite. I’m on the fence here but I kind of think the Rowston train stops here.

posts.body: Predictions: 13 of 13 Pending | Tied for 1st with 5134 others Della Maddalena vs. PratesCarlos Prates, TKO, R3I'm going to write this break down without making any CP jokes. I think JDM is the better technical boxer, and he's probably got the better grappling between the two guys here, but in terms of danger factor? It's Prates all day. Prates also showed that, despite smoking all those ciga-hets, he has surprisingly good cardio by going all five rounds with Ian Garry. I think Jack will win the minutes, but I think Prates will have the bigger moments. This fight is incredibly tough to break down. I'm violating my cardinal rule of trying to never pick against Australians on Australian fight cards, and I'm also going with a dog here, but Prates is just so dangerous. Might switch this pick. I could see JDM going for the takedowns and then staying on his bicycle and relying on the defensive boxing to win rounds, so I'm thinking about going JDM DEC here. But the range, the power, the momentum are all on Prates's side in a primarily striking matchup.Dariush vs. SalkilldQuillan Salkilld, TKO, R1Folk... what is this? This matchmaking is absolutely monstrous and potentially a violation of the Geneva Rights Convention. If this was a pure grappling match, I'd probably go with Benny, but even then, I don't think it would be a blowout because Quillan has showed he's pretty sharp on the mat in those fights with Gauge Young, Dom Mar Fan, and Yanal Ashmouz (RIP Ashmouz by the way, dude got cut for no reason). But Quillan's gonna get Benny out of there easily, man.Elliott vs. ErcegSteve Erceg, DECAlso another close fight, but WE rock with Michael "Astroboy" Scott here. Elliot's only loss in his last 5 is against Mokaev, and Erceg has not looked the same since he lost that close fight to Pantoja. But Erceg has a 9 year youth advantage and he doesn't have the same threat of getting colded in one shot that was there in the bouts with Ode Osbourne and KKF. Elliott also does not have the huge wrestling advantage that he had over Sumudaerji and Kai Asakura (RIP Asakura by the way).Gaziev vs. PeričićBrando Peričić, TKO, R1Unironically a close fight. Pericic is going to give Gaziev the exact sort of fight he wants by marching forward and constantly getting in Gaziev's face. We've seen how Gaziev struggles when you force him to be the aggressor and take the backfoot in the Rozenstruik fight, but Pericic is going to go for the kill immediately. And even though I don't think he hits as hard as Shamil, I think he's faster, he's got more tools in the toolbox, and he is ultimately the guy with more potential in this weight class. But he could easily get colded by a big meaty hook from the biggest and meatiest guy there is.Tuivasa vs. SutherlandLouie Sutherland, TKO, R1What are we doing here? This man was one win away from Jon Jones, and now I'm picking him to lose to a man who is 0-2 in the UFC and built like a bag of milk. Tai looked awful in that last fight. Yes, he managed to put some damage on Teixeira and nearly got a finish in R3, but I think that has more to do with Teixeira being a death-gasser. The first ten minutes were all Tuivasa getting outboxed and then taken down and controlled. Sutherland is... I don't know. Not good. He landed some nice right hands on Pericic and got some nice blast doubles but did nothing of note at all on top. Tuivasa will likely be able to get back to his feet, but I just know I can't trust Tai. I really hate that Sutherland is taking this fight on short notice just a month after taking a ton of damage against Pericic, but Tai did not look anything like the guy he used to be in that last fight, and it was against Teixeira who is just not good. I don't know. Tai's got a decent chin, and Sutherland's not much of a finisher, so it's possible that Tuivasa survives an initial onslaught and then gets his finish. Might switch.Rowston vs. BryczekCam Rowston, TKO, R2Idk bruh. This one's not as much of a blowout as the odds seem to suggest it will be. We're going with Rowston because he's got Ws over Brundage and Petroski? I mean, Petroski's chin is legendarily awful (dude TKO'd himself by shooting a takedown into Jacob Malkoun's hipbone), and Cody Brundage is Cody Brundage. That finish in the Brundage fight was pretty pathetic as well. It seemed like Brundage just gave up rather than actually getting TKO'd. But then again, Brundage went out right after the Rowston fight and nearly beat Donte Johnson, so who knows. So, basically, I don't know how to properly rate Rowston, but I'll still pick him because he looks like a decent striker and Bryczek lost to Ihor Poteria and then beat geriatric Brad Tavares who just lost to geriatric Eryk Anders right before Anders retired. MMA math doesn't always work, but uh, Rowston seems like the dude with more potential.Tafa vs. ChristianJunior Tafa, TKO, R1God, this is a foul, foul pick. I despise ever picking a Tafa, but realistically, what is Christian going to do here? His only hope is to takedown Tafa and get the submission, which I get he has shown the ability to do on his record, but most of those came from getting taken down himself and then throwing up a sub off his back. Tafa's not going to take Christian down. It's going to be on the feet for the most part, and Tafa has nearly finished most of his previous opponents because of his genuine skill in kickboxing. Christian got rocked badly from the first shot that Elekana landed on the chin, and I expect Tafa will also be able to find the chin. Either it's Tafa R1 KO or Tafa almost gets a finish, goes for ground and pound, ends up in an armbar, and gets submitted.Malkoun vs. MeerschaertJacob Malkoun, TKO, R1GM3 is done.Thicknesse vs. MoralesColby Thicknesse, DECMorales has had a hard run in his second UFC stint between Elijah Smith, Taylor Lapilus, and Raul Rosas, so there's an argument to be made that he can do well with this step down in competition. But Thicknesse hasn't had an easy run either. Aleksandre Topuria and then Josias Musasa, who he got the DEC over. The Topuria fight wasn't a complete blowout, either. Thicknesse was game for all 15 minutes and had brief moments of success. I've just seen more to inspire confidence from Thickie, and plus he's an Australian who's bound for decision on an Australian fight card.Johnston vs. SchultzJohnson, TKO, R1I've got zero knowledge about Johnston, but based on his record and the 22 seconds of tape I just watched, it seems like he's decent enough to beat Schultz. I get we all love Pinas and Pinas is a super powerful striker, but Pinas barely had to touch Schultz, and Schultz just crumbled. He's got decent wrestling, but he was able to do precisely none of it against Pinas, and Johnston showed in a couple fights that he can get up against the cage or at least prevent himself from being flatbacked. I think that's all he's going to need here.Gorimbo vs. MicallefMicallef, SUB, R2I wanted to believe in Themba Gorimbo or at least believe that he was good enough to beat Jeremiah Wells, but uh, it was not to be. He got outworked on the feet and nearly put into a couple of submissions. Micallef is no slouch with the BJJ or the kickboxing, so this should be pretty one-sided.Mar Fan vs. SteeleMar Fan, DECMar Fan is the sizable favorite here on Tap but a sizable dog in the odds. I'm still picking him, although I'm not loving it. Mar Fan looked very impressive against Frog Man last time around, and Frog Man is a decent fighter. I was most impressed in that fight by the hands Mar Fan showed and his cardio lasting all 15 minutes. Those both seemed like they would be issues based on his Road to UFC fights. In particular, Mar Fan looked totally unwilling to engage on the feet with that lanky black dude Yuri Ephoeviga, but then he was down to stand and bang with Mar Fan. So, my big concerns about Mar Fan both seemed to be irrelevant. Meanwhile, I'm not sold on Kody Steele. He beat a guy who was not UFC-level on DWCS, and then he got 30-27'd and rocked over and over again by Rongzhu. Rongzhu might be able to beat Mar Fan, too, but I don't think it would be by that huge of a margin. Also, Mar Fan can wrestle pretty well and does damage when he's in top control. Steele has a lot of grappling experience, but the only guy Mar Fan has been outwrestled by (and the only dude he's lost to in general) is Quillan Salkilld. And we just saw what Salkilld's other victim Gauge Young was able to do, so yeah. Plus, he's the Australian on the Australian card and likely headed for decision. * Edited at 04.26.2026, 12:37 PM ET *

posts.author: carloscanomx
posts.body: Predictions: 13 of 13 Pending | Tied for 1st with 5134 others Steele vs. Mar FanDom Mar Fan, DECFOTN incoming! Creo que arrancamos con una de las mejores peleas entre contendientes del peso ligero. DOM MAR FAN un peleador que le gusta utilizar el clinch para dañar, es creativo con codos y tiene muy buen pateo a la cabeza (question kick). Kody Steele, un atleta elite en grappling, entrevenando en Sindicate MMA buscando retomar lel camino de la victoria de visitante al otro lado del mundo. Fue algo difícil convencerme con el peleador con el que voy, la razón es simple, Kody Steele, es un peleador que es inteligente es agresivo, pero contra Rong Zhu batallo mucho con su distancia, lo cual creo que hará que tome un camino mas enfocado en lucha y grappling, el problema es que el peleador alto que tiene enfrente en el clicnh le gusta ser agresivo y dañar con golpes de poder. Creo que cualquiera puede saca ala victoria, pero a la vez creo que Steele, podrá mejorar su gameplan conforme avance la pelea, su esquina es muy concreta en acciones que buscan y kody las sigue. DOM podría dañar a Kody, si utiliza su jabugo como lo hizo en la final de RTF. Conclusión creo que tiene valor como underdog DOM MAR FAN, creo que debería llegar motivado en casa, debut en la UFC, un rival que buscará llegar con un gameplan basado en su juego, tiene jabugo que daña y podrá utilizar sus ventajas fisicas. Creo que estará mucho mas cerrada de lo que las lineas la marcan, entiendo a DOM MAR FAN como underdog, pero deberia estar en +100/+110 (2.0 o 2.10) no 2.62. PQNV

posts.author: Littledohyo
posts.body: Predictions: 13 of 13 Pending | Tied for 1st with 5134 others Della Maddalena vs. PratesCarlos Prates, TKO, R2I think a lot of people are looking at this fight and making their picks based on recent opponents, and that's fine. However, Burns fought with a clear desire to win against JDM that he never showed after that fight again. I just think it's sort of dumb to say stuff like 'lol he beat Burns so he's not that good', it's like saying Justin smashing Tony back in the day is a **** win. Obviously at a glance JDM had less relevant opponents, but that's also because he had to take a year off. Anyway, my reasons for the Prates pick are fairly simple. One, he didn't fade against Gary, and I think Gary is the best fighter at Welterweight right now, aside from possibly Islam. JDM can absolutely take the fight to Prates like Gary did and even push the pace faster and harder, but I just don't think he'll do the kind of damage he needs to put Prates out, and Prates has shown he can keep going. Two, JDM gets hit a lot in his fights from what I've seen. Against Belal, whatever, take the shots and keep rolling. Prates is one of the most powerful guys in the sport, and I think that's because of how he throws punches with his lank and bodyweight. JDM can't trade with him, I don't think, if the volume is even or maybe even in his favor. Three, JDM is coming off of a crushing loss that he even appears to be uncomfortable discussing in interviews. I don't like that, and maybe I'm just empathizing with his mindset or projecting, but all it takes is that flicker of hesitation in a full on striking matchup and you're toast. That's what we're talking about here with Prates, he's been smoking everyone and even had Gary afraid of him at the end of their fight. I don't think JDM is bad or incapable at all, but he's very much in a 'prove me wrong' situation right now. Having said all of that, when it comes to actual MMA and not striking - do YOU think JDM is going to shoot takedowns or try and clinch Prates against the fence like Leon did to win his first round EASILY with Prates? I certainly don't, and I think he should, and needs to do that. I think if JDM wins it will look like an absolute masterclass on the feet where he's elusive, lands 2-3 shots for every one Prates tries to land, or does land. This could so easily be that sort of fight, but twenty-five minutes with a guy that's proven he can keep going after eating big shots and taking a beating is too much. posts.author: Drew Dobers Chin
posts.body: Predictions: 12 of 13 Pending | Tied for 1st with 5134 others Della Maddalena vs. PratesCarlos Prates, TKO, R3Carlos Prates is such a menace with the striking that I think he's going to eat JDM's heart out in this fight. Realistically, JDM is relatively untested in deep waters per se as if we jump back to his title fight with Belal (which he only got because of Shavkat pulling out of the fight anyway), JDM and Belal went to war with both of their faces looking bruised up as hell because both of them rely on their combinations to do the work rather than their power so they're just throwing these dull punches for 25 minutes while neither one of them can find a finish. But with Carlos Prates stepping into the octagon against JDM, it's going to be a whole different story. Prates has some elite striking mixed with real knockout power which makes him even more dangerous. On top of that his fight IQ is great enough to keep JDM on the backfoot long enough to where he will set up that right hand. I have a feeling JDM is going to feel the fire very early in this fight and will try and wear the damage while just throwing a jab on the backfoot until Prates is able to track him perfectly into either another one shot shutoff or he'll get JDM backed up on the fence and tee off on him where JDM just sits down and taps out to strikes. There is no way where I see JDM finding this finish on Prates or him being able to be a real threat. I think the Prates ML is great here for a parlay piece if not Prates by KO.Dariush vs. SalkilldQuillan Salkilld, TKO, R1This almost feels like a setup, Dariush is such a veteran to the UFC and the sport and his wrestling is elite while Salkilld is a young prospect with an insane bag of tricks. I've got this weird feeling in my gut that Dariush manages to pull off a crafty vet decision here and can lay the gloves down and ride his horse off into the sunset but realistically, BSD showed that Dariush's chin has turned to dust and it's time for the guard change. I think Salkilld is gonna open this fight slowly and not rush the fight as Darisuh is going to kind of come out a little fast and start chopping the legs early but Salkilld is just going to throw some quick combination and shut off Dariush in the first 2 minutes. The size discrepancy is the main thing that really worries me for Darisuh. I think the Salkilld moneyline is solid for a small parlay booster or maybe take the -3.5 spread as Salkilld should win this fight everywhere.Gaziev vs. PeričićBrando Peričić, TKO, R1Pericic comes out like a bat out of hell and his only loss is when he gassed out against someone with a tungsten chin. Since the loss though, Pericic has been running right through every single opponent and I guarantee he's going to do it again against Shamil Gaziev. To be honest, Shamil is a complete meme fighter to me and he's just a blob with ko power. That Waldo loss really showed how bad his chin is and how he can succumb to pressure and fold faster than a lawn chair. All it took was a few jabs then Waldo sent his right hand and just shut Gaziev off. This fight should be super fast and probably end in the first 30 seconds once Pericic gets in the pocket and throws the entire kitchen at Gaziev and just drops him cold like Waldo did. I honestly think Pericic under 0.5 rds by ko will payout massive and if that doesn't hit, i'd be really really suprised.

posts.author: DevinM34
posts.body: Predictions: 13 of 13 Pending | Tied for 1st with 5134 others @Fast_Tulip Bryczek struggled with the length of Potieria though and Rowston is another guy who uses his length well. Bryczek does have fast hands and is dangerous early, so maybe he catches Rowston with a check left hook lunging in and gets the finish, but that's his only win condition imo. He fades after R1 and Rowston doesn't typically slow down. Another thing being overlooked too is Rowston's ability to wrestle and hold Bryczek down if he starts losing the exchanges on the feet. Tavares showed if you can get Bryczek down he doesn't really work to get back up. Rowston hasn't got to show it yet in the UFC but if you watch his regional tape he's got decent wrestling and an underrated ground game. He can easily win minutes with control here

posts.author: MrIzdik
posts.body: Predictions: 13 of 13 Pending | Tied for 1st with 5134 others The Bryczek/Rowlston fight for sure feels like one of the tougher fights to call. I keep flipping my pick. But one of the bigges things for me is Bryczek just feels like much more of a moment winner than minute winner. Losing a decision to Ihor is gross and he is 5-4 to the decision in his career. He just doesn't throw enough inbrtween his big moments. Rowlston is also 1-2 to the decision but at least his decisions are to decision merchants who are UFC level (Finney and Malkoun). And I kind of agree with Devin that if Bryczek doesn't find the KO than Rowlston can probably just wrestle him if he needs to. Bryczek KO only doesn't seem like a bad thing, but I feel like every time I pick the fighter who's only path is the KO I wind up losing
